hooks.prompt: Check whether this response completed a unit of work substantial enough to log to MEMORY.md (a shipped feature, a resolved bug, a meaningful decision, an approach that took more than two attempts). If yes, do NOT write to MEMORY.md yet — instead draft the one-block entry and surface it to the user with a short note like 'Worth logging to MEMORY.md? Reply "log it" to record this entry:' followed by the drafted block. Only invoke the decision-log skill to write once the user confirms. Rationale: an unconfirmed, model-generated memory entry becomes context the agent reads every future session, and machine-generated context measurably degrades agent performance; a human-confirmed entry does not. If the response was a one-line answer, a clarifying question, an interrupted thought, or trivial work, do nothing. Be conservative — frequent low-value proposals are noise. (To restore the old silent-append behavior, replace 'do NOT write yet / surface for confirmation' with 'invoke the decision-log skill to append' — see README, 'Tune the Stop hook'.)

If the user's request involves implementation work that resembles past tasks in this project (bug fix, architecture choice, library selection, performance work, refactor), silently invoke the errors-check skill in read mode before suggesting any approach. Surface a match only if confident it applies. Skip this check for one-line answers, clarifying questions, or non-implementation requests.
